witnesses.description: A point inside a rational triangle by explicit convex weights.
The line x + y = 1 separates the triangle from (2,2).

checks.claim: The listed point is the listed convex combination of the triangle vertices.
checks.notes: The validator checks nonnegative weights, weight sum one, and the exact weighted vector sum.

checks.claim: The listed hyperplane separates the finite triangle from the outside point.
checks.notes: The validator recomputes all dot products and checks that every vertex is on the bounded side while the outside point is strictly beyond the threshold.

checks.claim: The listed tight vertex indices are exactly the finite face supported by x + y = 1.
checks.notes: The validator recomputes the tight indices from the separator scores.

checks.claim: The listed convex weights also produce point (1/2, 1/3).
checks.notes: Exact convex-combination replay computes (1/3, 1/3), so the malformed x-coordinate 1/2 leaves error 1/6. The separate qf-lra-bad-convex-combination-point row owns the proof-object refutation.

checks.claim: The fixed QF_LRA conflict point_x_error = 1/6 and point_x_error = 0 is unsatisfiable.

data.certificate: The resource regression parses the source SMT-LIB artifact, emits UnsatFarkas evidence, and independently checks the certificate.

checks.notes: This proof row isolates the false convex-combination x-coordinate as a small QF_LRA/Farkas contradiction after replay computes the point.

checks.claim: The outside point (2,2) also satisfies x + y <= 1.
checks.notes: Exact replay computes outside_score = 4, so the malformed outside_score <= 1 claim leaves excess 3. The separate qf-lra-bad-separator row owns the proof-object refutation.

checks.claim: The fixed QF_LRA conflict outside_score = 4 and outside_score <= 1 is unsatisfiable.

data.certificate: The resource regression parses the source SMT-LIB artifact, emits UnsatFarkas evidence, and independently checks the certificate.

checks.notes: This proof row isolates the false separator bound as a small QF_LRA/Farkas contradiction after replay computes the outside score.

checks.claim: General hyperplane separation and duality theorems require future Lean reconstruction.

data.target_theorem_shape: strict/weak hyperplane separation for convex sets, Farkas theorem as duality, and Hahn-Banach-style functional separation
data.future_checker: Lean module with no sorryAx dependencies plus exact rational certificate bridges for finite-dimensional special cases

checks.notes: The finite rows check only one listed rational hull and separator; general separation and duality remain proof-assistant work.
